Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Interpretation Act and to make related amendments to other Acts
Identification.ShortTitle: An Act to amend the Interpretation Act and to make related amendments to other Acts
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Interpretation Act and to make related amendments to other Acts

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Interpretation Act to provide that Acts of Parliament and regulations are to be construed as upholding the Aboriginal and treaty rights of Indigenous peoples recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982, and not as abrogating or derogating from them. It also amends or repeals similar provisions in other Acts known as non-derogation clauses.
His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: The Interpretation Act is amended by adding the following after section 8.2:

Subsection.Text: Every enactment is to be construed as upholding the Aboriginal and treaty rights of Indigenous peoples recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982, and not as abrogating or derogating from them.
For the purposes of subsection (1), Indigenous peoples has the meaning assigned by the definition aboriginal peoples of Canada in subsection 35(2) of the Constitution Act, 1982.

Section.Text: Section 2.3 of the Fisheries Act is repealed.
Section 21.1 of the International Boundary Waters Treaty Act and the heading before it are repealed.
Section 2.2 of the Canadian Navigable Waters Act is repealed.
Subsection 2(3) of the Canada Wildlife Act is repealed.
The heading before section 2 of the English version of the Canada Petroleum Resources Act is replaced by the following:
Section 3 of the Act is repealed.

Heading.TitleText: Canada–Newfoundland and Labrador Atlantic Accord Implementation Act

Section.Text: The heading “Interpretation” before section 47 of the English version of the Canada–Newfoundland and Labrador Atlantic Accord Implementation Act is replaced by the following:
Section 48 of the Act is repealed.

Heading.TitleText: Canada-Nova Scotia Offshore Petroleum Resources Accord Implementation Act

Section.Text: The heading “Interpretation” before section 49 of the English version of the Canada-Nova Scotia Offshore Petroleum Resources Accord Implementation Act is replaced by the following:
Section 50 of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 2(3) of the Migratory Birds Convention Act, 1994 is repealed.
Subsection 2(3) of the Firearms Act is repealed.
The heading before section 2 of the Oceans Act is replaced by the following:
Section 2.1 of the Act is repealed.
Section 3 of the Canada Marine Act and the heading before it are repealed.
Subsection 5(2) of the Mackenzie Valley Resource Management Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: This Act is to be construed as upholding the Aboriginal and treaty rights of Indigenous peoples recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982, and not as abrogating or derogating from them.
For the purposes of subsection (2), Indigenous peoples has the meaning assigned by the definition aboriginal peoples of Canada in subsection 35(2) of the Constitution Act, 1982.

Section.Text: Section 4 of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 is repealed.
The heading before section 2 of the English version of the Canada National Parks Act is replaced by the following:
Subsection 2(2) of the Act is repealed.
The heading before section 2 of the English version of the Yukon Act is replaced by the following:
Section 3 of the Act is repealed.

Heading.TitleText: Canada National Marine Conservation Areas Act

Section.Text: Subsection 2(2) of the Canada National Marine Conservation Areas Act is repealed.
Section 3 of the Species at Risk Act is repealed.
Section 3 of the First Nations Fiscal Management Act and the heading before it are repealed.

Heading.TitleText: First Nations Oil and Gas and Moneys Management Act

Section.Text: Section 3 of the First Nations Oil and Gas and Moneys Management Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (d), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (c) and by repealing paragraph (e).

Heading.TitleText: An Act to amend the Canadian Human Rights Act

Section.Text: Section 1.1 of An Act to amend the Canadian Human Rights Act is repealed.

Heading.TitleText: Ending the Captivity of Whales and Dolphins Act

Section.Text: Section 6 of the Ending the Captivity of Whales and Dolphins Act and the heading before it are repealed.
Section 3 of the Indigenous Languages Act is repealed.

Heading.TitleText: An Act respecting First Nations, Inuit and Métis children, youth and families

Section.Text: Section 2 of An Act respecting First Nations, Inuit and Métis children, youth and families is repealed.
Section 3.1 of the Oil Tanker Moratorium Act and the heading before it are repealed.
The heading before section 2 of the Impact Assessment Act is replaced by the following:
Section 3 of the Act is repealed.
Section 3 of the Canadian Energy Regulator Act is repealed.

Heading.TitleText: United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act

Section.Text: Subsection 2(2) of the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act is repealed.

If Bill C-21, introduced in the 1st session of the 44th Parliament and entitled An Act to amend certain Acts and to make certain consequential amendments (firearms), receives royal assent, then section 72.1 of that Act and the heading before it are repealed.
If Bill C-35, introduced in the 1st session of the 44th Parliament and entitled the Canada Early Learning and Child Care Act, receives royal assent, then section 3 of that Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (2) to (5) apply if Bill C-49, introduced in the 1st session of the 44th Parliament and entitled An Act to amend the Canada–Newfoundland and Labrador Atlantic Accord Implementation Act and the Canada-Nova Scotia Offshore Petroleum Resources Accord Implementation Act and to make consequential amendments to other Acts (in this section referred to as the “other Act”), receives royal assent.
If section 2 of the other Act comes into force before section 8 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the heading before section 8 and sections 8 and 9 of this Act are deemed never to have come into force and are repealed;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the heading “Interpretation” before section 47 of the English version of the Canada–Newfoundland and Labrador Atlantic Accord Implementation and Offshore Renewable Energy Management Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: section 48 of that Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: If section 2 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 8 of this Act, then that section 2 is deemed to have come into force before that section 8 and subsection (2) applies as a consequence.
If section 108 of the other Act comes into force before section 10 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the heading before section 10 and sections 10 and 11 of this Act are deemed never to have come into force and are repealed;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the heading “Interpretation” before section 49 of the English version of the Canada–Nova Scotia Offshore Petroleum Resources Accord Implementation and Offshore Renewable Energy Management Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: section 50 of that Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: If section 108 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 10 of this Act, then that section 108 is deemed to have come into force before that section 10 and subsection (4) applies as a consequence.
